notes: v1.14.128 - UI sweep RULE F (cards share one .gx/card vocabulary per surface) - closes the A-F sweep. Audit: on Command, .nba/.lens/.lbtn use radius 16 + filter:saturate(1.40) + gradient bg + 1px ACCENT border + accent inner-glow; the Work/Ref grid .rf-card matches (radius 16, accent --rfac). The Ask card was the lone outlier: it rode the .gx grid recipe (radius 14, no saturate, fixed cyan-10% inner glow) so its shell didn't match its Command neighbors (the .117 fix had aligned only its layout/typography, not the shell). Fix: dropped 'gx' from the Ask button markup and gave .ask the Command card recipe with its VIOLET accent - border-radius:16px, filter:saturate(1.40), gradient bg, 1px color-mix(--vio 42%) border, inset glow color-mix(--vio 14%), margin 0 16px 14px (matches .nba/.lens). Accent COLOR stays per-card (violet) by design; only the vocabulary (radius/saturate/glow/margin) is unified. Browser-verified: Ask radius 16 == NBA 16, filter saturate(1.4) == NBA, no .gx class. CSS + one markup class, redesign-only (#pg-cmd New-UI) -> ?legacy=1 byte-identical. node --check x3 + sw.js, brace gate, three-stamp lockstep. UI consistency sweep A-F now complete (A .122-124, B .125, C .126, D audit-only, E .127, F .128). DEVICE-VERIFY: the Ask card reads as part of the Command surface (same rounding/depth/glow as the NBA card, violet accent); ?legacy=1 unchanged.
